arts.ttl: Arsenal player’s agents ‘lay foundations’ for January exit – Travelling for transfer talks
arts.sum: Arsenal’s Jakub Kiwior has been linked with an exit from the Emirates repeatedly in recent months. Napoli have been presented as one of the most interested clubs, and it’s been reported they’ve made an offer worth around €15m for the Poland international. That wasn’t entertained by Arsenal, who are said to want €10m. Il Mattino, The post Arsenal player’s agents ‘lay foundations’ for January exit – Travelling for transfer talks appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Alexander Isak transfer truth revealed as Premier League rule key to £115m Arsenal deal
arts.sum: Newcastle United could lose Arsenal -linked Alexander Isak as they remain under pressure to sell players to avoid breaching Premier League spending rules. Having been linked with a move to Arsenal over the past few transfer windows, there has been some talk that the Gunners could go back in to sign Isak.

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Mikel Arteta comments on potential January plans
arts.sum: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta suggests that the Gunners are already considering how they can strengthen their squad during the January transfer window.

arts.ttl: Four Arsenal academy defenders not involved in today's U19s clash, could be involved v Monaco in CL tonight
arts.sum: Arsenal have a number of defensive injury doubts ahead their Champions League meeting with Monaco on Wednesday night. Gabriel Magalhaes, Jurrien Timber, Oleksandr Zinchenko, Riccardo...

arts.ttl: Arsenal are 'new Tottenham' and they must sign Alexander Isak to solve woes
arts.sum: Former Arsenal, Tottenham and Chelsea defender Gallas told Prime Casino : "It's always the same problem with Arsenal; they put in good performances and win against big teams and then drop points when it really matters, when they had the chance to close the gap on Liverpool. Having also dropped against Brighton, Bournemouth and Newcastle earlier in the season, Gallas believes he now sees the Gunners as the new Tottenham due to their inconsistent nature.

arts.ttl: Why have Arsenal come to rely on set pieces?
arts.sum: Arsenal's last three goals have all come from corners, Sky Sports' Peter Smith explains the reasons why their open-play stats have dropped off and the importance of set-pieces for Mikel Arteta.

arts.ttl: Arsenal and Tottenham could miss out on £12m wonderkid as Premier League rivals confirm he's close to new deal
Every word Mikel Arteta said on Partey injury, Tierney return, Arsenal creativity and Jesus future
arts.sum: I’m very pleased with that, the way the team is adapting and the way players are making the effort to play in different positions. So when we face an incredibly good team like Monaco who is doing really well we are so used to it because we play opposition like that every week.

arts.ttl: Automatic Champions League progression will hand Arsenal huge bonus and a worst-case scenario
arts.sum: Arsenal are closing in on a Champions League knockout spot despite two full rounds of matches being left to play. The final group phase table will not be completed until the end of January, but with only one game to go before Christmas, things are looking up for Mikel Arteta.

arts.ttl: Arsenal full squad revealed for Monaco as Gabriel Magalhaes and Jurrien Timber decisions made
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta and Arsenal once more face a nervous wait over a host of key players with injuries continuing to cause major issues. Arteta must balance those ambitions with an increasingly difficult Premier League picture that has seen Chelsea move above his side and into second - after Fulham managed a draw with Arsenal on Sunday.
